headline: Attribute certificates now decode their RFC 5755 attribute values and attribute-certificate extensions, not just the certificate structure.
summary: pki.schema.attrcert.parse previously surfaced each attribute's value and each extension as raw bytes; it now decodes them. The six RFC 5755 section 4.4 attribute-value syntaxes are decoded to structured values: the role (RoleSyntax -- role authority and role name), the clearance (policy id, the security-classification bit list, and security categories), the service authentication information and access identity (SvceAuthInfo -- service, identity, and optional auth info), and the group and charging identity (IetfAttrSyntax -- policy authority and the octet/oid/utf8 value list). The five section 4.3 attribute-certificate extensions are decoded too: the audit identity, the target information and proxy information (Targets -- a target name, target group, or target certificate, the last reusing the IssuerSerial decoder), the no-revocation-available marker, and the AA controls (path-length constraint, permitted and excluded attribute types, and the permit-unspecified flag). Each attribute value and extension is surfaced additively on the parsed certificate (a decoded field alongside the raw bytes); an unknown attribute type or extension id is preserved opaque so the parse never fails on an unrecognized entry, while a recognized-but-malformed value -- a wrong context tag, a non-canonical default, an empty required field, a GeneralName that does not validate -- fails closed with a typed error.

Added: pki.schema.attrcert.parse decodes the RFC 5755 section 4.4 attribute values -- role (RoleSyntax), clearance (Clearance, including the classification bit list and security categories), authenticationInfo and accessIdentity (SvceAuthInfo), and group and chargingIdentity (IetfAttrSyntax) -- and the section 4.3 attribute-certificate extensions -- auditIdentity, targetInformation and proxyInfo (Targets, with targetCert reusing the IssuerSerial decoder), noRevAvail, and aaControls. Each is surfaced additively (a decoded field alongside the raw value) through the parse consumer path; an unrecognized attribute type or extension id is preserved opaque, and a malformed recognized value fails closed with a typed error. Every GeneralName inside these structures is validated through the shared name decoder.
